title: soldr-toolchain catalogue v1
description: Flat sha-bearing asset catalogue consumed by soldr's runtime resolver (crates/soldr-cli/src/fetch/manifest_lookup.rs in zackees/soldr). One row per (owner, repo, tag, asset) tuple. Mirrors the shape of the legacy asset-index.json schema_version=5 produced on the assets branch by scripts/build_asset_index.py, with a fresh v1 namespace so the catalogue can evolve independently of the v5 asset-index. See docs/ASSET_CATALOG.md for the directory + URL convention this catalogue references.

schema_version.description: Hard-pinned to 1. Bump for breaking changes; consumers MUST refuse unknown versions.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the producer wrote the document. Diagnostic only — consumers ignore it for routing decisions.

origin.description: Optional self-URL of the catalogue (e.g. https://zackees.github.io/soldr-toolchain/catalogue.v1.json). Lets cached copies prove what origin they came from.

entries.description: Flat list of asset rows. Order is producer-controlled; consumers MUST treat the array as an unordered set keyed by (owner, repo, tag, asset).

owner.description: GitHub owner (user or org) — follows GitHub's account-name rules: 1-39 chars, alphanumeric + hyphen/underscore, no leading hyphen.

repo.description: GitHub repo name. For locally-hosted vendored blobs the owner+repo is the toolchain repo itself (zackees/soldr-toolchain).

tag.description: Upstream release tag (e.g. v1.12.11) OR the literal string 'assets' for blobs hosted on the soldr-toolchain assets branch.

asset.description: Asset filename byte-for-byte as the upstream / vendor stored it. Used as the lookup key in soldr's resolver.

url.description: Direct download URL. MUST be on a github.com / *.githubusercontent.com / zackees.github.io origin. The producer (scripts/build_asset_index.py) emits one of three URL shapes — see docs/ASSET_CATALOG.md 'URL convention'.

sha256.description: Lowercase hex sha256 of the asset bytes. Consumers MUST verify this on download. Producers compute it locally for vendored blobs and accept upstream-provided values for GitHub-release assets (verified at first-fetch and cached).
